key_insight: The paper gives the first finite-time convergence guarantee for the original Adam algorithm in nonsmooth, nonconvex optimization, including bias correction and no extra modifications.
engineering_relevance: This strengthens the theoretical basis for using Adam in practical deep learning settings where objectives are often nonsmooth and noise can be heavy-tailed.
method: Analyzes Adam through the Online-to-Nonconvex Conversion framework.
Keeps the classical Adam update with bias correction intact.
Uses a randomly scaled learning rate and assumes β1 = β2.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a provable convergence rate of 1/T^(2/13) for nonsmooth nonconvex optimization, including the heavy-tailed noise regime.
watch_item: The guarantee is derived under the specific parameter choice β1 = β2 and a randomly scaled learning rate, so direct applicability may depend on matching these conditions.
paper_title: Adam Converges in Nonsmooth Nonconvex Optimization
